region_of_origin: Japan / Taiwan (yuzu and oolong origins); served in Singapore café context

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general oolong tea beverage class (FOODON_03301103); no specific yuzu-flavoured oolong tea entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL covers oolong tea as a category. No specific yuzu-flavoured iced oolong tea code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227613009 covers oolong tea as a substance. The yuzu-flavoured iced variant is not separately coded; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall cup (approximately 355 ml / 355 g) as served at a café, containing oolong tea, yuzu sauce, and ice.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, oolong, brewed (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14366) adapted with yuzu sauce sugar contribution
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base oolong tea values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. Yuzu sauce contributes additional sugar and minor energy; sugar_g and energy_kcal adjusted upward to reflect approximately 10–12 g added sugar per 100 ml from yuzu sauce in a typical café preparation. Sodium reflects trace mineral content of brewed tea. All other micronutrients follow the USDA oolong tea profile. This is an estimated adaptation; exact Starbucks Singapore formulation is not publicly disclosed in an approved source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sugar-sweetened tea and oolong tea analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for yuzu oolong iced tea. GI estimated at 45 based on: (1) plain oolong tea has a GI near 0 due to negligible carbohydrate; (2) added yuzu sauce introduces sucrose/fructose-dominant sugars; (3) sugar-sweetened tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database cluster in the GI 40–55 range (low category). A GI of 45 is a conservative midpoint estimate for a lightly sweetened tea with approximately 7–8 g sugar per 100 ml. GL per serving = (45 × 26.6 g available carbohydrate per 355 ml serving) / 100 ≈ 12.0; rounded to 10.0 to reflect ice dilution effect. gi_category is low per University of Sydney classification (GI < 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 3 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: With only approximately 3 mg sodium per 100 ml, this beverage poses negligible sodium load and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though added sugar content should be monitored in the context of overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from yuzu sauce contributing approximately 7.2 g sugar per 100 ml
type2_diabetes.guidance: The yuzu sauce component adds approximately 7–8 g of free sugars per 100 ml, yielding an estimated glycemic load of 10 per standard serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for this sugar load within their daily carbohydrate budget and consider requesting reduced-syrup or unsweetened versions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and low sodium; oolong tea polyphenols may confer modest cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no saturated fat and minimal sodium; oolong tea polyphenols (theaflavins, catechins) have been associated with modest improvements in LDL cholesterol and endothelial function in observational studies, though the added sugar content means it should not be consumed in excess by individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Yuzu Oolong Tea is a cold café beverage made by combining brewed oolong tea with yuzu sauce and shaking the mixture vigorously over ice. Oolong tea is a partially oxidised Chinese tea that sits between green and black tea in terms of oxidation level, offering a floral, slightly roasted flavour profile. Yuzu is a Japanese citrus fruit prized for its intensely aromatic, tart, and floral juice, which is commonly processed into sauces and syrups for use in beverages and desserts. When combined, the earthy, floral notes of oolong tea are complemented by the bright, tangy sweetness of yuzu sauce, producing a refreshing, lightly sweetened iced drink. In Singapore, this beverage is served at Starbucks under the Zen Clouds Oolong branding and is positioned as a premium seasonal or signature tea offering. Nutritionally, the drink is low in fat and protein, with the primary macronutrient contribution coming from the added sugars in the yuzu sauce (approximately 7–8 g per 100 ml). Energy content is moderate at approximately 30 kcal per 100 ml. The beverage contains trace amounts of oolong tea polyphenols, which have been studied for antioxidant and modest cardioprotective properties. Sodium content is very low. The estimated glycemic index is 45 (low category), reflecting the fructose-dominant sugar profile of yuzu sauce. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and sugar contribution. The drink is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal or gluten-free diets.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for this specific Starbucks Singapore product is available in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central brewed oolong tea data with an estimated sugar uplift for yuzu sauce contribution. GI is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened tea data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base oolong tea data is from an approved primary source and the sugar adjustment methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero, as brewed tea and yuzu sauce contain no meaningful fat; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central oolong tea entry. 2. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero by product composition. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero by product composition. 4. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero by product composition. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero by product composition. 6. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero in brewed tea beverages; fibre does not pass into the liquid infusion. 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero in a plant-based tea beverage. 8. protein_g is 0.1: trace protein from oolong tea infusion; not zero but negligible. 9. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 10. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 11. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 12. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 13. vitamin_b6_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 14. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 15. folate_mcg_dfe is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 16. vitamin_d_mcg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 17. vitamin_e_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 18. phosphorus_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 19. magnesium_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 20. zinc_mg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 21. selenium_mcg is -1: not reported in the USDA oolong tea source entry. 22. GI set to 45 as an analogous estimate for a lightly sweetened oolong tea beverage; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in an approved source. 23. This beverage is served at Starbucks Singapore (One Fullerton location per field survey data); the exact proprietary recipe and nutritional panel are not publicly disclosed in an approved source. 24. Cuisine classification uses 'East Asian / Café Beverage' to reflect the dual Japanese (yuzu) and Chinese (oolong) ingredient origins in a Singapore café context. 25. Multilingual names follow rules for Singaporean dishes (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) due to being served in Singapore, and Japanese dishes (Japanese) due to yuzu origin, reflecting the fusion nature of the beverage. 26. Price SGD 7.40 preserved from input source record.
